title: hieracium hirsutum subsp. hirsutum
intro: Hieracium hirsutum subsp. hirsutum, also known as hairy hawkweed, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenoid compounds. The health benefits of hairy hawkweed include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit potential antiviral and antifungal activities. Hairy hawkweed can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
